description: git-file-list-dirty-raw returns M:filepath for a modified tracked file
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Modify a tracked file
Run git-file-list-dirty-raw
Verify output contains 'M:<filename>'

description: git-file-list-dirty-raw returns A:filepath for a new untracked file
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Create a new untracked file
Run git-file-list-dirty-raw
Verify output contains 'A:<filename>'

description: git-file-list-dirty-raw returns D:filepath for a deleted tracked file
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit containing a file
Delete the tracked file
Run git-file-list-dirty-raw
Verify output contains 'D:<filename>'

description: git-file-list-dirty-raw returns empty output for a clean directory
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Run git-file-list-dirty-raw
Verify output is empty

description: git-file-list-dirty-raw accepts a path argument and lists dirty files in that path
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Add a linked worktree at a separate path
Modify a tracked file inside the worktree
Run git-file-list-dirty-raw from the main repo with the worktree path as argument
Verify output contains the modified file

description: git-directory-dirty-count returns 0 for a clean Worktree
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Run git-directory-dirty-count
Verify output is '0'

description: git-directory-dirty-count returns correct count for modified files
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit containing 2 tracked files
Modify both files
Run git-directory-dirty-count
Verify output is '2'

description: git-directory-dirty-count counts staged files
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Create a new file and stage it with git add
Run git-directory-dirty-count
Verify output is '1'

description: git-directory-dirty-count counts untracked files
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Create a new file without staging it
Run git-directory-dirty-count
Verify output is '1'

description: git-directory-dirty-count accepts a path argument and counts from that path
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Add a linked worktree at a separate path
Modify a tracked file inside the worktree
Run git-directory-dirty-count from the main repo with the worktree path as argument
Verify output is '1'

description: git-worktree-list-raw output has 7 fields per line
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Add a linked worktree
Run git-worktree-list-raw
Verify each output line has exactly 6 ▮ separators (7 fields)

description: git-worktree-list-raw dirtyCount field is 0 for a clean Worktree
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Add a linked worktree with no uncommitted changes
Run git-worktree-list-raw
Verify the 3rd field (dirtyCount) is '0'

description: git-worktree-list-raw dirtyCount field reflects uncommitted changes in the Worktree
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit containing a tracked file
Add a linked worktree
Modify the tracked file inside the worktree
Run git-worktree-list-raw from the main repo
Verify the 3rd field (dirtyCount) is '1'

description: git-worktree-list-raw ahead field is a plain integer
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Add a linked worktree and make 2 commits on that branch
Run git-worktree-list-raw
Verify the 4th field (ahead) is '2' (not 'ahead 2')

description: git-worktree-list-raw behind field is a plain integer
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Add a linked worktree, then add 3 commits to main
Run git-worktree-list-raw
Verify the 5th field (behind) is '3' (not 'behind 3')

description: COLOR_ALIAS_GIT_WORKTREE_DIRTY is defined with value 21
steps: Source the color theme in a zsh session
Echo $COLOR_ALIAS_GIT_WORKTREE_DIRTY
Verify output is '21'

description: git-worktree-list shows dirty count column when a Worktree has uncommitted files
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit containing a tracked file
Add a linked worktree
Modify the tracked file inside the worktree
Run git-worktree-list from the main repo
Verify output contains '±' or the dirty count number

description: git-worktree-list hides dirty count column when all Worktrees are clean
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit
Add two linked worktrees with no uncommitted changes
Run git-worktree-list
Verify output does not contain '±'

description: git-file-edit with no argument opens dirty files in the current directory
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit containing a tracked file
Modify the tracked file
Run git-file-edit (dry-run or mock nvim)
Verify the modified file path is passed to nvim

description: git-file-edit with a path argument opens dirty files from that Worktree
steps: Create a temp git repo with an initial commit containing a tracked file
Add a linked worktree
Modify the tracked file inside the worktree
Run git-file-edit from the main repo with the worktree path as argument
Verify the full path to the modified file is passed to nvim
